Simple, Transparent Pricing

Understand v0 pricing in 2026: free access, token-based paid usage, model tiers (Mini/Pro/Max), and team options.

Last reviewed April 2026. v0 now uses token-based billing and model tiers, so always confirm current limits on the official pricing page.

Free

Good for first projects and testing v0 prompts before committing to paid usage.

$0 /mo
  • Starter usage allocation
  • Core UI generation features
  • Basic export workflow
  • Great for initial experimentation
Start for Free
Most Popular

Paid Usage

Token-based usage for creators shipping production features more frequently.

Varies /mo
  • Token-based billing
  • Higher generation capacity
  • Priority workflow features
  • Private project support
  • Access to advanced models
View Paid Options

Team

Collaboration-focused setup for teams that iterate across product and engineering.

Varies /seat
  • Everything in paid usage
  • Shared team workspace
  • Version history
  • Collaborative workflows
  • Admin dashboard
Start Team Trial

Business

Security and governance capabilities for larger product organizations.

Custom /team
  • Everything in Team
  • SSO (Single Sign-On)
  • Audit logs
  • Dedicated support
  • SLA guarantees
Contact Sales

Enterprise

Tailored solutions for large organizations with specific needs.

Custom
  • Everything in Business
  • On-premise deployment
  • Custom model fine-tuning
  • White-label options
  • Dedicated account manager
Contact Sales

Frequently Asked Questions

How does token-based billing work in v0?

v0 usage is metered by tokens, which typically vary based on prompt complexity, chosen model tier, and output length. If your plan limit is reached, you can usually wait for reset windows or move to a higher-capacity paid option.

What is the difference between Mini, Pro, and Max models?

Model tiers generally trade speed and cost against output depth. Mini is typically suited to simpler iterations, Pro balances quality and efficiency for everyday work, and Max is better for complex generation tasks where output quality matters most.

Can v0 support full-stack workflows and GitHub export?

Yes, current v0 workflows include Git-connected iteration and runtime-oriented testing patterns for broader app delivery. For production systems, you should still validate backend security, data modeling, and deployment architecture in your own environment.

Should I trust static pricing screenshots from old guides?

No. Pricing and model access can change quickly, and many older pages still reference pre-2026 credit systems or old branding. Use this page for context, then verify final numbers directly on the official v0 pricing page before making plan decisions.
